content.problemStatement:
You are given integers NNN, KKK and an array BBB of length NNN.

Count the number of arrays AAA, also of length NNN, which satisfy the following conditions:


1≤Ai≤K1 \le A_i \le K1≤Ai​≤K
The lexicographically largest†^{\dagger}† subsequence‡^{\ddagger}‡ of A[1,i]A[1, i]A[1,i] has a length of BiB_iBi​.

A[1,i]A[1, i]A[1,i] represents the prefix of the array containing iii elements, i.e. [A1,A2,…,Ai][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i][A1​,A2​,…,Ai​].
Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

†^{\dagger}† A sequence XXX is said to be lexicographically larger than YYY if either of the following conditions holds:


YYY is a prefix of XXX but X≠YX \ne YX=Y.
The first index iii where Xi≠YiX_i \ne Y_iXi​=Yi​ satisfies Xi>YiX_i > Y_iXi​>Yi​.
‡^{\ddagger}‡ A sequence XXX is a subsequence of YYY if it is possible to obtain YYY by deleting any number (possibly zero) of elements from YYY without changing the order of the remaining elements.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid arrays AAA modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Both the sum of NNN and the sum of KKK over all tests does not exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : All arrays of length 111 with 1≤A1≤10001 \le A_1 \le 10001≤A1​≤1000 is valid. There are 100010001000 such arrays.
Test Case 2 : The valid arrays are [2,1],[1,1],[2,2][2, 1], [1, 1], [2, 2][2,1],[1,1],[2,2]. However, [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is not valid because for the prefix of length 222, the lexicographically largest subsequence is [2][2][2] which has length 111.
Test Case 3 : The only valid array is [1,2][1, 2][1,2].
